raw_consumer_statement:
I received a bill for my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX from Citi back around XX/XX/XXXX. The problem was, I'd not used this card in several months and it had been paid in full from my prior usage. I contacted the carrier and disputed the charges. They sent me a new card. I threw the card in my wallet. It was never activated. 

The next month, I received another bill. The account had been used again. I called and disputed the new charge. I was sent a new card. I was advised that somebody was able to go into a XXXX XXXX across the country from where I live, input my SSN and use the account that way. The representative then blocked this ability for future use with SSN lookup only. 

I received notices advising my fraudulent charges were cleared. 

The next month, I received another bill. This time, I'm told it's for late fees. Late fees on what? I am told that this will be straightened out. 

The next month, yet another bill. And another. 

I have called, and called. Once I called and was advised by a collections agent that the fraud dept had determined that there was no evidence of fraud. The next day, I spoke with the fraud dept who advised that this was not true, that they would complete their investigation, then fix my account and update my credit report to reflect this rather than the late payments they're now showing. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I called and spoke with a representative who advised that my fraudulent charges had been removed. I advised there's still a balance that's showing delinquent. She advised that they're reopening the fraud claim and submitting a balance review. This should take 7-10 business days. The charges will be removed and my credit will be restored. I was told I can disregard the balance. 

It's XX/XX/XXXX. I have received a notice that Citi is going to submit my account to collections. There's no charge on that account that is mine. At this point, I don't believe they have any intent to fix this problem. Not sure why they wouldn't, but I've thought this for the last 6-7 months. Just sure that every time I spoke to them would be the last.
